source: MorphMarket Morphpedia / Ball Python Genetics Database v3 (mid-2026), 230 base traits

genes.notes: Distinct line of Hypo; does not complement other Hypo/Ghost lines.
Breeder reports inconsistent production.
Non-complementary with Jolliff/MJ/TSK/VPI lines.
Non-complementary with GCR/MJ/TSK/VPI lines.
Non-complementary with GCR/Jolliff/TSK/VPI lines.
Same allele as Black Axanthic (confirmed by RGI genetic testing, 2024). Non-complementary with GCR/Jolliff/MJ/VPI.
Non-complementary with GCR/Jolliff/MJ/TSK lines.
Same allele as Axanthic (TSK) — confirmed by RGI genetic testing 2024.
Candy and Toffee are the same allele (independently discovered/named).
Female sub-fertility/high infertility rate; kinking; photophobia.
Allelic to Clown; reclassified from Incomplete Dominant to Recessive (Oct 2020).
2023 RGI testing revealed DG is actually 3 combined genes (DGa/DGb/DGc); some lines incompatible with others.
Visually similar to but distinguishable from Desert Ghost.
Distinct project-specific Ghost line, not identical to Vesper dominant gene.
Genetically distinct/unrelated to standard TYR-gene Albino. OCA2 gene deletion.
Status unconfirmed/controversial as standalone trait; linked to Piebald.
Possibly linked to Splatter (unconfirmed).
Suspected allelic to Ultramel (per breeder Rance Meier).
Possibly same gene as Moray (disputed).
Possibly same gene as Monsoon (disputed).
Related to Graziani Hypo, Ghost, Blue Ghost, Green Ghost, Bell, Butterscotch, Citrus Hypo.
Reclassified to Recessive (Dec 2019).
Unrelated to the combo 'Rainbow Banana' — shared name only.
Reclassified from Incomplete Dominant to Recessive (Dec 2019).
Reclassified Recessive Dec 2020.
Caused by TYRP1 gene variants.
Legitimacy as true genetic recessive is debated/unconfirmed.
Originally misidentified as Incomplete Dominant before being proven recessive.
Removes clutter from yellows and adds orange tones.
Sex-linked (X-chromosome). Females produce Female Makers; males produce Male Makers.
Masks genetic wobble found in other Spider Complex members.
Super Black Pastel has duckbill deformity and rare kinking.
2025 RGI confirmed Butter and Lesser are genetically DISTINCT despite historical grouping.
Light-to-severe wobble in all animals. Super Champagne is LETHAL. Avoid pairing with Sable, HGW, Spotnose.
Super Cinnamon commonly has duckbill deformity and rare kinking.
Proven distinct from Gulf Coast Vanilla in 2015.
'Het' here denotes the incomplete-dominant heterozygous form, not a recessive het.
Light-to-severe wobble in single-gene animals. Super HGW is LETHAL. Genetically separate from Woma despite name.
Possibly synonym for High Intensity OD (unconfirmed).
Genetic test available for this trait.
Status disputed — no line conclusively proven genetic/heritable in ball pythons.
'Acts Like Super' form is called Whiplash. Breeding Whiplash produces only Jolt and Black Pastel offspring.
Genetically unrelated to Woma despite the name.
Commonly combined with Fire and other BlkEL-complex genes.
Super Lesser individuals sometimes show enlarged protruding eyes. 2025 RGI confirmed Lesser and Butter are genetically DISTINCT.
Related to but distinct from Scaleless Head (confirmed separate mutations).
Only one wild Mojave has ever been found; all captive Mojaves descend from that single animal.
In 2023 some sellers attempted rebranding as 'Carnage', largely rejected by the community.
Single-gene animals can look brighter/more orange than supers, historically causing misidentification.
Super Pixel proven in 2020 (14 years after project start). Do not confuse with Granite gene.
Super Raven pairing produced runt twins that died; not successfully established.
Allelic to Black Pastel; combining the two produces 'Black Razor' (black snake with silver dorsal stripe).
Suspected allelic to Chocolate (8 Ball Complex).
Super Sable displays a wobble. Sable x Spider combinations difficult to hatch and show severe wobble.
Related to but distinct from Microscale (confirmed separate mutation).
Part of the Yellow Belly allelic series.
Documented light-to-severe neurological wobble. Super Spider is LETHAL. Some expos have banned sale.
Complex genetics still being studied. Possibly linked to Migraine (unconfirmed).
Super Spotnose (Powerball) tends to show a wobble. Spotnose combined with Champagne or Spider shows severe wobble.
Corrected: Incomplete Dominant (NOT recessive — error from early databases).
Subtle trait, easily confused with Normal or Fire.
Documented genetic wobble varying from almost unnoticeable to apparent. Super Woma (Pearl) produced by NERD in 2000.
Proved incomplete-dominant by Josh Jensen, 2022.
Originated from X-treme Gene stock but proven a distinct gene.
Breeder notes ~32% visual offspring ratio from het-like pairings (previously suspected polygenic).
Considered a problematic morph: poor growth, poor breeding performance, facial/cranial malformations.
Female fertility/reproduction issues documented.
Breeder describes as 'Dominant with no Super'. Fast-growing, aggressive, good feeders.
Controversy: multiple incompatible lines exist; many originally-labeled animals proved to be Normals.
Only one wild Pinstripe ever found; all hobby animals descend from that single specimen.
Not to be confused with the recessive 'Sunset' morph.
Connected to Microscale mutation (only observed on Microscale animals so far).
More of a descriptive look than a fixed single-gene trait; heritable/provable in some lines.
Denotes any phenotype not yet proven genetic through breeding trials. High risk/gamble category.
Not reliably heritable in a predictable single-gene manner.
Not a single gene; a category tag applied to cross-species animals.
Not heritable in the traditional sense. Arises from somatic mutation or embryo fusion.
Unusual sex-linked-style inheritance pattern, still being studied.
Frequently confused with the separate Ringer Mark trait.
Never proven to be a reliably inherited trait on its own; distinct from the Ringer gene.
Geographic locality population (Volta region, Ghana/Togo), not a mutation. Notable for large clutch sizes and large adult size.
